page_content:
Internships

TLPL 479G (https://app.testudo.umd.edu/soc/search/? courseId=TLPL479G) (Field Experiences in Education; Internship) Taken in the spring of the junior year, this course is the third of ﬁve ﬁeld-based professional experiences for students who have been accepted into the Elementary Education Teacher Preparation Program. Interns will assist the mentor teacher in the planning, delivery and assessment of instruction and become familiar with the various roles a teacher plays in the teaching-learning process. The internship is an integral component of this semester and includes three immersion weeks in addition to the two full school days each week in a public elementary school classroom.

TLPL 489G (https://app.testudo.umd.edu/soc/search/? courseId=TLPL489G) (Internship in Education: Elementary Education Residency) Taken in both the fall and spring of the senior year, this course is the fourth and ﬁfth of ﬁve ﬁeld-based professional experiences for students who are degree seeking in the Elementary Education Teacher Preparation Program. Residents will participate throughout the year in all aspects of a teacher's life in the school. In the fall semester, Resident Interns are beginning to practice the art and craft of teaching under the supervision of a mentor teacher. In the spring semester (student teaching) Resident Interns gradually assume full responsibility for classroom management, planning, delivery of instruction and assessment with continual guidance from a mentor teacher. Teacher candidates complete the yearlong internship in a network of professional development school settings (PDS Networks). These sites reflect the linguistic, ethnic and economic diversity that is characteristic of the Greater Baltimore-Washington D.C. region. For additional information about the yearlong internship, see the College of Education entry.
